courses.purpose: This is a first course in freshman calculus. It aims to provide the foundational mathematical tools needed in engineering disciplines and to strengthen analytical and problem-solving abilities.
courses.description: This course covers functions, limits and continuity, derivatives and their applications, indefinite and definite integrals, trigonometric, logarithmic, exponential and hyperbolic functions and their inverses, techniques of integration, applications of the definite integral (area, volume and arclength), indeterminate forms, L'Hopital's rule, and improper integrals.
learning_outcomes: It is equipped with the necessary Engineering Mathematics knowledge in specific areas of engineering disciplines.
Gain analytical thinking ability.
Problem solving skills develop.
They can apply mathematical knowledge to engineering problems.

courses.name: TURKISH I
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the relationship between language and perception, and to develop awareness of Turkish as a language. It also supports students in learning basic academic communication skills such as analyzing texts, summarizing, taking notes, and writing reports.
courses.description: Learning to link language and perception, understanding the structural characteristics of Turkish, learning to analyze texts, to summarize, take notes and write reports.

Recognizes the place of language in social life and knows the differences in language use according to context.
Learns the place of Turkish among the languages of the world. Recognizes the differences in origin and structure of languages in the world.
Learns about the historical periods and development of Turkish. Recognizes the alphabets used by Turks during the journey.
Learns about the studies conducted on the Turkish language during the Republic period. Learns about Mustafa Kemal Atatürk's views on language unity.
Recognizes the sound characteristics of Turkish. Can distinguish words that entered Turkish from foreign languages. Recognizes and evaluates proverbs, idioms, reduplications and dialect elements.
Learns about the basic functions of language; its role in the processes of developing thought, communicating and transferring information.
Understands the importance of language in effective communication.
Acquires basic comprehension skills (listening-reading).

courses.name: LIFE SCIENCES AND COMPUTER ENGINEERING
courses.purpose: This course introduces the integration of computer engineering with the life sciences and shows why this interdisciplinary perspective is important. It aims to build awareness of biological principles and their relevance to modern computing applications in medicine and biosciences.
courses.description: A survey of biological principles covered in a range from cell to genetic engineering. Integration of computer engineering and the life sciences, with a foundation in biology, chemistry and bioengineering methods. Introduction to biochemistry, biosystems, human metabolism and genetics. Modern evolutionary theory and animal/plant systems. Biological data and information flow. Application of computer engineering techniques to problems arising in biosciences.
learning_outcomes: Be informed about basic life sciences.
Understand the importance of computer engineering in this field.
Learn modern computer applications in medicine.
Be able to disseminate the applicability of computer applications in life science.

courses.name: Linear Algebra
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop students' analytical and problem-solving abilities through the study of linear algebra concepts and methods. It also supports the application of engineering mathematics to science and engineering problems.
courses.description: This course contains these concepts: R Space, Dot Product of Vectors, Projections, Equations of Line and Plane, Systems of Linear Equations and Solutions, Gauss-Jordan Elimination, Matrices, Transpose and Inverse of Matrix, Matrix Applications, Determinants and Properties, Eigenvalue of Matrices, Cross Product, Scalar Triple Product, Norm, General Vector Spaces, Subspaces, Linear Dependence and Independence, Span, Basis and Dimension, Inner Product Spaces, Gram-Schmidt Orthogonalization Process, Linear Transformations, Kernel and Range, Rank and Nullity, Matrices of Linear Transformations, Eigenvectors of Linear Transform, Diagonalization, Change of Basis, Similarity, Jordan Canonical Form, Quadratic Surfaces, Complex Vector Spaces, Complex Matrices.
learning_outcomes: Increase the awareness of his mental potential and abilities; gain an ability to utilize a higher percentage the capacity of the mind via brain exercises.
Acquire effective analytical thinking ability and study habits; and also deepen and widen his thought and vision.
Develop problem-solving skills.
Becomes able to apply their knowledge of Engineering Mathematics to science and engineering problems.
Acquires basic information that can be set the decision model.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, System of Linear Equations and Solutions
Hafta 5: Cross Product, Scalar Triple Product and Properties. Lines and Planes. The Vector Spaces R2, R3, Rn and Norm of a Vector.
Hafta 7: Linear Dependence, Linear Independence, Spanning, Basis and Dimension
Hafta 9: Inner Product Spaces, Gram-Schmidt Process
Hafta 11: Kernel and Range Spaces, Rank and Nullity, Inverse Linear Transformations
resources: Howard Anton, Chris Rovves, Elementary Linear Algebra, Applications Version (1994), John Wiley&Sons.
David C. Lay, Linear Algebra and Its Applications (2000), Addison - Wesley.

courses.name: TURKISH II
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable students to use their native language adequately and correctly, to think about a topic in detail, and to express their thoughts in a rational, simple, original, and effective way in written and oral form. It also aims to develop habits of listening, criticizing, planned thinking, respecting ideas, and speaking in front of an audience.
courses.description: Turkish Language II is an active learning course covering writing rules, punctuation, speech disorders, the use of Turkish in social media, scientific writing preparation methods, literary genres, textual analysis, writing petitions/minutes/reports, discourse design, and prepared and impromptu speaking techniques.
learning_outcomes: Learns how to use language appropriately, including spelling rules and punctuation marks.
Will learn to recognize speech disorders and how to avoid them.
Identify language errors in daily speech and social media.
Learn what to consider when preparing scientific writing. They learn about Scientific Research, Citing Sources, Creating a Bibliography, and Writing Styles.
Will be able to recognize literary genres and analyze texts.
Learn how to write petitions, minutes and reports.
Become familiar with the functions of language and become able to design discourse appropriate to the context.
Will learn about prepared and impromptu speaking techniques.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introducing the course syllabus and providing information about the course/Writing Rules
Hafta 6: Use of Turkish in social media (digital texts, blog posts, advertisements, etc.)
Hafta 7: Scientific Writing Preparation Methods (Scientific Research, Citing Sources, Creating a Bibliography, Writing Styles)
Hafta 10: Textual analysis (story, poem, essay, etc.)
Hafta 11: Textual analysis (story, poem, essay, etc.)
Hafta 13: Discourse design (Language and its functions)
Hafta 14: Prepared and impromptu speaking techniques
resources: Üniversiteler İçin Türk Dili ve Kompozisyon, Başkent Üniversitesi Yayınları (2008), Ankara
Türk Dil Kurumu İmla Kılavuzu, TDK Yayınları: 525. (2008), Ankara
Türkçe Sözlük, TDK Yayınları (2006), Ankara

courses.name: DATA STRUCTURES
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to fundamental data structures and the principles behind their design and use. It also helps students develop the ability to analyze algorithm complexity and apply data structures effectively in problem solving.
courses.description: Basic data types. Arrays, singly and doubly linked lists, memory considerations, stacks and recursion, queues, tree structures and traversal methods, sorting and searching algorithms, space and time considerations, and applications of data structures.
learning_outcomes: Learn dynamic memory allocation and linked list concepts
Learn basic data structures
Learn how to implement data structures
Learn data abstraction and object oriented design concepts
Get the ability to analyze the complexity of algorithms
Comprehend recursion
Get practice on solving problems with data structures
Gains the ability to work in interdisciplinary projects.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Data Structures, Programming Principles
Hafta 4: Pointers and Dynamic Memory Management
Hafta 5: The List Container and Iterators
Hafta 9: Queues / Priority Queues and Their Applications
Hafta 13: Advanced Associative Structures - I (Hash Functions)
Hafta 14: Advanced Associative Structures - II (2-3-4 Trees, Red/Black Tree Structures)
resources: Data Structures with C++ using STL, 2nd Edition, W.Ford & W.Topp, Prentice-Hall, 2002.
Data Structures and Algorithm Analysis in C++, 3rd Edition, M.A. Weiss, Addison Wesly Inc., 2006.
Data Structures & Program Design in C, 2nd Edition, Kruse, Tondo, Leung, Prentice-Hall, 1997.

courses.name: ATATÜRK'S PRINCIPLES AND THE HIST. OF THE TURKISH REVOLUTION I
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the principles of Atatürk and the history of the Turkish Revolution, and to help students understand the historical background of the Turkish Republic. It also seeks to develop awareness of the political, social, and ideological developments that shaped modern Turkey.
courses.description: Principles of Atatürk and History of Revolution I course, outlined the qualifications of the Ottoman Empire, the internal and external reasons of the collapse of the Empire, the policies of the Western industrialized states against the Ottoman Empire in the 19th century also the national dimension of the war of independence and the development of compulsory causes of war are described in detail.
learning_outcomes: Explain Principles of Atatürk and main concepts related to Revolution history.
Explain the concepts of Reform/Revolution.
Describe the concept of National Forces.
Explain the concepts of Republic/Democracy.
Recognize the concept of Ideology.
Explain the main points of the period related to Turkish War of Independence and foundation of the Turkish State.
Explain the developments at Ottoman Empire before Turkish Revolution.
Describe the World War I and its results.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Ataturk's Principles and History of Revolution objectives of teaching courses, Revolution and Revolution concepts, ideas about Atatürk, the Turkish Revolution, the main features of the Turkish Revolution.
Hafta 2: Internal and External Causes of the Collapse of the Ottoman Empire, Tanzimat and Constitutional Periods, Intellectual Movements in Constitutional Era
Hafta 3: Tripoli, Balkan, I. World Wars and the Armistice.
Hafta 4: The condition of the country against the occupation , Useful and Harmful Societies, The arrival of Mustafa Kemal to Samsun.
Hafta 5: M. Kemal's decision-National Struggle, Amasya Circular, Erzurum and Sivas Congresses.
Hafta 6: Amasya and Sivas Meeting, the Board of Representation to arrive in Ankara, The acceptance of National Pact.
Hafta 7: The opening of National Assembly , internal revolts, their causes and consequences.
Hafta 8: Turkish-Soviet relations, the Eastern Front, the Treaties of Moscow and Kars
Hafta 10: The Armenian Question, the Turkish-Armenian War and the Treaty of Gümrü
Hafta 11: South Side-Ankara Agreement, the Western Front-I. İnönü, the London Conference, II. İnönü, Eskişehir-Kütahya Wars.
Hafta 12: Battle of Sakarya, Mudanya Armistice.
Hafta 14: Resolutions and Decisions of the Lausanne Treaty and evoluation of its decisions according to National Pact.

courses.name: DIGITAL LOGIC DESIGN
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the basic concepts of digital logic and enable them to apply these concepts to engineering problems. It also introduces digital logic-based circuit design and the fundamental elements used in digital logic.
courses.description: Analog and Digital system definitions, applications, benefits, Binary Systems. Conversion among binary systems, coding systems, logic gates. Boolean Algebra rules. Minimization of logic functions. Combinational Logic. Flip Flops and Synchronous Sequential Logic. Registers and Counters. Memory and Programmable Logic.
learning_outcomes: To learn the basic concepts of digital logic and to implement to engineering applications.
To learn Digital logic based circuit design.
Acknowledgement of basic elements used in digital logic.
To convert real word logic problems to digital logic problems, to determine input and output variables, to design and conversion.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Digital Systems, Numbers And Number Systems, Codes, Error Detection Codes
Hafta 2: Digital Systems, Numbers And Number Systems, Codes, Error Detection Codes
Hafta 3: Error Detection Codes, Boolean Algebra And Boolean Arithmetic's, Digital Logic Gates
Hafta 4: Error Detection Codes, Boolean Algebra And Boolean Arithmetic's, Digital Logic Gates
Hafta 5: Gate Level Minimization, Karnaugh Map, NAND/NOR Implementation, XOR/XNOR Gates
Hafta 6: Gate Level Minimization, Karnaugh Map, NAND/NOR Implementation, XOR/XNOR Gates
Hafta 7: Combinational Circuits, Analysis And Design, Adders and Subtractor Circuits, Binary Multipliers, Coders And Decoders.
Hafta 9: Synchronous Sequential Logic, Flip-Flops, Analysis And Design Of Clocked Sequential Circuits, Level Minimization
Hafta 10: Synchronous Sequential Logic, Flip-Flops, Analysis And Design Of Clocked Sequential Circuits, Level Minimization
Hafta 11: Registers, Shift Registers, Ripple Registers, Synchronous Registers
Hafta 12: Registers, Shift Registers, Ripple Registers, Synchronous Registers
Hafta 13: Memory And Usage, Read Only Memory, Programmable Logic Array
Hafta 14: Memory And Usage, Read Only Memory, Programmable Logic Array
resources: Mano Morris, Digital Design, 3/E, ISBN: 013062121-8, Prentice Hall, 2002
Floyd Thomas L., Digital Fundamentals, 8/E, ISBN: 8177587633, Prentice Hall, 2002

courses.name: ADVANCED ENGLISH II
courses.purpose: This course is an Academic English course at C1 level. It aims to develop students’ reading, listening, speaking, and writing skills, while also integrating productive skills with critical thinking and improving lexical and structural competence.
courses.description: ENG 200 is an Academic English course at C1 level, whose pre-requisite course is ENGE199. This course essentially focuses on the development of four language skills: reading, listening, speaking and writing. Moreover, it intends to integrate the development of productive skills with critical thinking skills. Enhancing students' efficiency in terms of lexical and structural points is also a crucial component of the course. Learners are encouraged to study independently.
learning_outcomes: develop skills and sub-skills of reading, listening, speaking and writing.
learn the meaning and use of language associated with different text types, using appropriate strategies to work out their meaning.
develop their critical thinking skills.
write different types of academic essays by means of process writing and following the rules of academic writing.
have prepared and delivered a mini presentation about a topic related to their field of study.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the Course, Book 1 Unit 1 What is Engineering?, Unit 2 Shapes, Unit 3 Materials, Unit 4 Tools
Hafta 2: Book 1 Unit 5 Energy, Unit 6 Simple Machines, Unit 7 Working with Numbers, Unit 8 Types of Measurement
Hafta 3: Book 1 Unit 9 The Scientific Method, Unit 10 Safety Precautions, Unit 11 Civil Engineering
Hafta 4: Book 1 Unit 12 Chemical Engr. , Unit 13 Mechanical Engr., Unit 14 Electrical Engr, Unit 15 Aerospace Engr.
Hafta 5: Book 2 Unit 1 History of Engr. , Unit 2 Traits of an Engineer, Unit 3 An Engineer?s Education, Unit 5 Problem Solving
Hafta 6: Book 1 Unit 1 What is Engineering?, Unit 2 Shapes, Unit 3 Materials, Unit 4 Tools
Hafta 7: Book 2 Unit 4 Presenting Information, Unit 6 Creativity, Listening Exercises
Hafta 9: Book 2 Unit 7 Tables and Graphs, Unit 8 Dimensions and Drawings, Unit 9 Materials and Properties , Unit 10 Working with Numbers, How to prepare a short presentation
Hafta 10: Book 2 Unit 13 Industrial Engineering, Unit 14 Software Engineering, Book 3 Unit 1 Newton?s Laws, Unit 2 Laws of Thermodynamics, Unit 3 Rate Processes
Hafta 11: Book 3 Unit 4 Statics and Dynamics, Unit 5 Electricity, Unit 6 SI System of Units
Hafta 12: Book 3 Unit 7 Engineering Design Method, Unit 10 Statistics, Unit 11 Computer Engineering
Hafta 13: Book 3 Unit 8 Models, Unit 15 Biomedical Engineering
Hafta 14: How to read graphs, Listening Exercises
resources: Lloyd, C., & Frazier, J. A. (2011) Career Paths: Engineering Student's Book (Tenth Impression-2021). Express Publishing.

courses.name: ECONOMICS
courses.description: Basic concepts. Supply and demand model, flexibility and usage areas, demand curve and consumer behavior, supply curve and firm behavior, interaction of individuals in markets, risk analysis and decision making, change of companies and industries over time, monopoly, product differentiation, monopolistic competition and oligopoly, anti -monopoly policies and regulation, labor markets, taxes, transfers and income distribution, sustainable development.
learning_outcomes: To have knowledge about economics issues in order to understand the effects of engineering solutions in global and aggregate economies.
To have knowledge about Risk Analysis and Decision Making.
To have information about contemporary economic problems.
Understands the basic principles of macroeconomics.
Explain the causes of economic problems and form economic models.
Interpret finance and monetary policies.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Markets and How Markets Work
Hafta 6: Risk Analysis and Decision Making
resources: Taylor, J.B. (2004) Economics, Houghton Mifflin
Çolak, Ö. F. (2008), İktisat

courses.name: Discrete Structures
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide the mathematical foundations needed for computer engineering. It helps students develop logical and algorithmic thinking and prepares them to model and solve computational problems using discrete methods.
courses.description: Logic. Logical operations and laws of logic, quantifiers and proofs of theorems. Sets. Subsets and operations on sets. Principles of counting. Inclusion-exclusion and pigeon-hole principles. Integers, mathematical induction. Relations and functions. Operations on relations. Partially ordered sets. Lattices. Boolean algebras. Graphs. Euler and Hamiltonian paths and circuits. Trees. Binary trees, spanning trees. Tree searching. Elements of coding theory.
learning_outcomes: Represent the propositions using logical connectives and interpret symbolic expressions.
Determine is a mathematical argument correct or not.
Develop the knowledge on recursively-defined structures
Gain the ability to think algorithmically.
Have theoretical knowledge on Equivalence relations and Partial orders; solve related application problems.
Learn the basic principles of counting and advanced counting techniques.
Obtain information about basic concepts, methods and applications of graph theory.
Apply discrete structures and related methods to solve problems in Computer Engineering.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Logical operations and laws of logic, quantifiers and proofs of theorems
Hafta 4: Subsets and operations on sets
Hafta 10: Partially ordered sets and Lattices
Hafta 13: Euler and Hamiltonian paths and circuits
Hafta 14: Trees, Binary trees, spanning trees, Tree searching, Elements of coding theory
resources: Rosen K.H., Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications, 6/E, McGraw-Hill, 2007
Kolman B., Busby R.C., Ross S., Discrete Mathematical Structures, 5/E, Prentice Hall, 2004
Grimaldi R.P., Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics, 5/E, Addison Wesley, 2003

courses.name: INTRODUCTION TO ELECTRONICS
courses.description: Circuit Variables. Circuit elements and methods of analysis of resistive networks. Circuit theorems and equivalent models. Analysis of RL, RC and RLC circuits. Dynamic Circuits Analysis and solution methods. AC circuits and steady-state power analysis.
learning_outcomes: Be able to learn the basics of electronic circuits, apply and solve engineering problems.
Have embedded system programming techniques to run electronic projects and programming knowledge to control them.
Have the ability to set up the experimental setup of an electronic circuit and/or problem given to them, and to perform measurements and analyzes on their own.
Have the ability to design and implement a complex electronics project as a group that is used to solve real world problems.
Learn how to search the literature and prepare a project report and have the experience of presenting their group projects in front of the community.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction, Presentation and Distribution of Project Topics
Hafta 7: DC Biasing ? BJT,Introduction to Electronic Development Boards
Hafta 9: Introduction to Field Effect Transistors-FET Embedded System Programming - Processing
Hafta 10: DC Biasing-FET Electronic Sensors, Wireless Data Transmission Modules
Hafta 11: Analysis of Various Field Effect Transistor Circuits
Hafta 12: FET- Question Solutions Electronic Circuit Design and Printed Circuit Preparation with Fritzing

Hafta 14: Evaluation of Projects ? Presentation Week

courses.name: ATATÜRK'S PRINCIPLES AND THE HIST. OF THE TURKISH REVOLUTION II
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students understand the philosophy behind Atatürk's Principles and the efforts of the new Turkish State to establish a contemporary identity and state structure. It also examines the political, historical, domestic, and foreign policy developments that shaped the Republic of Turkey.
courses.description: In this course, students study the formation of the new Turkish State, the principles and reforms of Atatürk, and the historical, political, and social developments that influenced the Republic of Turkey. The course covers constitutional developments, political reforms, legal, educational, cultural, social, and economic reforms, foreign policy in the Atatürk and İnönü periods, and the complementary principles of Atatürk.
learning_outcomes: Explain Principles of Atatürk and their importance.
Explain the effects of the developments that take place in Europe and Throughout the World on Turkish Republic.
Explain the effects and results of European and World politics on Turkey and the results of them.
Describe the effects of Capitalism/Emperialism on Turkey.
Explain the relations / problems between Turkey and its neighbours.
Explain the significance of Turkey in Europe and throughout the
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Atatürk Period-Multi-Party System in Transition (Republican People's Party, the Progressive Republican Party, the Free Republican Party), abolition of the Caliphate)
Hafta 2: Turkish Revolution Counter Revolution (Sheikh Said Rebellion, Delivery of Calm Act II. Period Establishment of the Independence Tribunals, Izmir assassination, Menemen Event)
Hafta 3: Constitutional Movements in Turkey (1876 Constitution, the 1921 Law of Fundamental Organization, the 1924 Constitution, the 1961 Constitution, the 1982 Constitution)
Hafta 4: The new Turkish State Formation u, Political Reforms, (Sultanate Kaldırılıması, Proclamation of the Republic, Abolition of the Caliphate)

Hafta 5: Revolutionary Movements in the Field of Law, Education and Culture Revolutionary Movements (Law on Unification of Education, Letter revolution, Turkish History and Turkish Language Institutions

Hafta 6: Revolutionary Movements in the social field (dervish lodges and retreats Off, Hats revolution, the Surname Law, Women's Rights, the International Measurement and Adoption of the figures), Revolutionary Movements Economic Area.

Hafta 7: Revolutionary Movements in the social field (dervish lodges and retreats Off, Hats revolution, the Surname Law, Women's Rights, the International Measurement and Adoption of the figures), Revolutionary Movements Economic Area.

Hafta 9: Foreign Policy of Turkey in the period of 1932-1938 (the Turkish-Soviet relations, the Turkish-Italian relations, Turkish-German relations, the Turkish-British relations, Montreux Straits Convention, the Turkish-French Relations and Hatay problem)
Hafta 10: After Atatürk's Turkey Turkey-İnönü Period Domestic and Foreign Policy, II. World War II,
Hafta 11: Principles of Atatürk, the Turkish revolution on the complementary principles (National Independence, Sovereignty, National Union), Republicanism and Nationalism.
Hafta 14: Evaluation of the period, with related topics Documentary Screening

courses.name: COMPUTER ORGANIZATION
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental concepts of computer organization and architecture, and helps students understand how computers are structured and operate. It also builds the basis for later study of memory systems, I/O organization, operating systems, and assembly language.
courses.description: Concepts of organization and architecture. Historical evolution of computers. General structure and operation of computers. Interfacing, bus structures and PCI, memory, cache and cache organization. DRAM, SRAM, ROM, error correction, DRAM organization, magnetic disk and disk organization. Optical disk structures. I/O architecture, programmed I/O, interrupt driven I/O, DMA, I/O channels and processors. Operating system. Scheduling. Memory management, virtual memory. Arithmetic Logic Unit, types of arithmetic operations and number representation. Machine language instructions. Data types, operations. Assembly language.
learning_outcomes: Know concepts of organization and architecture. Historical evolution of computers and general structure and operation of computers.
Informed of interfacing, bus structures, cache and cache organization. DRAM, SRAM, ROM, DRAM organization.
Gather optical disk structures, I/O architecture, programmed I/O, interrupt driven I/O, DMA, I/O channels and processors.
Learn operating system, scheduling, memory management and virtual memory.
Apply arithmetic logic unit, types of arithmetic operations and number representation.
Gain an insight on machine language instructions, data types, operations, and assembly language.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Concepts of organization and architecture
Hafta 3: General structure and operation of computers
resources: Stallings, Computer Organization and Architecture: Designing for Performance: International Version, 8th Edition, 2010, Pearson Higher Education, ISBN-10: 0135064171

courses.name: Probability and Statistics
courses.description: Basic principles of probability. Definition and basic theorems of probability. Concept of a random variable. Probability distributions. Mathematical expectation. Some discrete and continuous distributions. Joint probability distributions. Random sampling. Some important statistics. Sampling distribution. Sampling distributions of sample means and sample variances. One- and two-sample estimation problems. One-and two-sample tests of hypotheses.
learning_outcomes: Interpreting statistical data sets by using numerical and graphical techniques.
Learning the probability definition and its rules to be able to solve problems.
Learning the concept of random variables and their probability and distribution functions.
Choosing suitable probability distributions to apply and interrelate with the real life problems.
An ability to describe the basic concepts of statistics (population, sample, random sample, sampling distribution etc.)
Using sampling distributions in confidence intervals and sampling distributions, gaining ability of statistical inference.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Definition and basic theorems of probability.
Hafta 3: Concept of a random variable.
Hafta 6: Some discrete and continuous distributions.
Hafta 12: Sampling distributions of sample means and sample variances.
Hafta 13: One- and two-sample estimation problems.
Hafta 14: One-and two-sample tests of hypotheses.
resources: Walpole R.E., Myers R.H., Myers Sh. L., Ye K. Probability and Statistics or Engineers and Scientists Prentice Hall, 7/E, 2002.
Akdeniz F., Olasılık ve İstatistik, Nobel Kitabevi, 13. Baskı, 2007.
Erbaş S. O., Olasılık ve İstatistik, Gazi Kitabevi, 2007.

courses.name: MATHEMATICS FOR COMPUTER ENGINEERING
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the mathematical methods needed to model and solve engineering problems, especially those arising in computer engineering. It also supports the development of analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
courses.description: Ordinary Differential Equations (ODE): First order ODE, Linear differential equations of second order, Systems of ODE, Series solutions of ODE. Fourier Series. Partial Differential Equations. Complex Numbers and Functions. Numerical Methods in Linear Algebra. Numerical Methods for Differential Equations.
learning_outcomes: Learn methods for solving Ordinary Differential Equations.
Know Laplace Transforms and apply them to solve differential equations of engineering subjects.
Learn Fourier Series, Integrals and Transforms.
Recognize and solve partial differential equations.
Apply numerical methods in Linear Algebra and Differential Equations.
Learn complex functions.
Develop problem-solving skills and working together.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Ordinary Differential Equations (ODE): First order ODE
Hafta 2: Linear differential equations of second order
Hafta 3: Linear differential equations of second order
Hafta 11: Numerical Methods in Linear Algebra
Hafta 12: Numerical Methods in Linear Algebra
Hafta 13: Numerical Methods for Differential Equations

courses.name: OPERATING SYSTEMS
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental concepts, mechanisms, and structure of operating systems. It aims to help students understand how operating systems manage processes, resources, and concurrency, and to prepare them for system-level programming.
courses.description: Basic modes of operating system, its mechanisms and structure. Processes and resources. Models. Threads. Inter-process communication. Mutual exclusion and synchronization. Concurrency models. Semaphores and monitors. Classical IPC problems. Deadlocks, its detection and recovery, the Banker's algorithm. CPU scheduling. Preemptive and non-preemptive strategies. Memory management. Swapping. Virtual memory, page table and paging algorithms.
learning_outcomes: Learn fundamental concepts of operating systems
Understand and explain the mechanisms of system processes
Understand concurrency
Get practice on developing system-level programs

Describe and apply normalization rules.

courses.name: COMPUTER NETWORKS
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the fundamental concepts, architectures, and protocols of computer networks. It also helps students understand how networked systems operate and how data communication is organized across layers.
courses.description: Fundamentals and architecture of computer networks, computer network topologies and types, OSI Model and network protocols, Physical Layer, Data Link Layer, Network Layer, Transport Layer, Session Layer, Presentation Layer, Application Layer. LAN communication technologies (802.X family and Ethernet, token ring, FDDI), Network communication systems, Network communication, network setup and management, services provided on the network, TCP/IP and Internet structure subnetting, IP routing, internet communication via e-mail, receiving and sending files over the internet, using FTP program, DHCP, DNS, UDP, protocols, Internet, Intranet, extranet structures.
learning_outcomes: Knows basic network concepts and network structures, including multi-layered network architecture model and the main protocols used.
Explains the equipment used in data communication and networks and their operations.
Defines the working principles of widely used Internet applications.
Knows the processes related to secure and unsafe data transfer, TCP protocol, UDP protocol in the transmission layer services.
Knows routing algorithms and IP Protocol, IP addressing issues in network layer.
Explains and compares Ethernet technologies at the Data Link Layer level, including error detection and correction methods, and local area network layers.

increase their language proficiency by using appropriate vocabulary, sentence structures, and transitions to make their presentations more impactful and understandable.

courses.purpose: Bu ders, öğrencilere bulut hesaplama alanının temel mimarilerini, bileşenlerini ve kullanım alanlarını tanıtmak için tasarlanmıştır. Ayrıca SaaS, PaaS ve IaaS gibi temel hizmet modellerini ve bunların işletmeler için stratejik kullanımını kavratmayı amaçlar.
courses.description: Cloud Computing architectures, components and tools. Software as a Service architecture, components and advantages. Software as a Service (SaaS) case study. SaaS Technologies. Platform as a Service architecture, components and advantages. Platform as a Service case study. PaaS Technologies. Infrastructure as a Service architecture, components and advantages. Infrastructure as a Service case study. IaaS Technologies. Developing IT Cloud Computing strategies for small, medium and large enterprises. High Performance Computing Technologies. Throughput computing Technologies. Cluster computing Technologies. Map Reduce Framework case study with Hadoop. Hadoop use cases. Open Source Software technologies for Cloud Computing. Workload characterization techniques. Performance optimization. Cloud Computing research topics.
learning_outcomes: Learn Cloud Computing architectures.
Learn Cloud Computing components and tools.
Learn SaaS, PaaS, IaaS technologies.
Identify examples of cloud computing technologies.
Learn IT Cloud Computing strategies for small, medium and large enterprises.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Cloud Computing architectures, components and tools.
Hafta 2: Software as a Service architecture, components and advantages.
Hafta 3: Software as a Service (SaaS) case study. SaaS Technologies.
Hafta 4: Platform as a Service architecture, components and advantages.
Hafta 5: Platform as a Service case study.
Hafta 6: PaaS Technologies. Infrastructure as a Service architecture, components and advantages.
Hafta 7: Infrastructure as a Service case study.
Hafta 10: Developing IT Cloud Computing strategies for small, medium and large enterprises.
Hafta 11: High Performance Computing Technologies. Throughput computing Technologies.
Hafta 12: Cluster computing Technologies. Map Reduce Framework case study with Hadoop. Hadoop use cases.
Hafta 13: Open Source Software technologies for Cloud Computing. Workload characterization techniques.
Hafta 14: Performance optimization. Cloud Computing research topics.

Hudzia B., Cloud Computing: Principles and Paradigms, Wiley, 2011

courses.name: SOFTWARE ARCHITECTURES
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental concepts and approaches of software architectures. It aims to equip students with the ability to analyze, design, evaluate, and apply architectural styles, patterns, and development approaches in software systems.
courses.description: Introduction to software architecture. Architectural modeling. Architectural styles. Architecture description languages. Analyzing and reviewing software architectures. Architectural patterns and frameworks. Component-oriented development. Architecture-based development. Service-oriented architectures. Model-driven development. Aspect-oriented development.
learning_outcomes: Describe general concepts and approaches of software architectures.
Analyze and design software architectures.
Know and apply architectural design patterns and frameworks.
Know and apply service-oriented architecture model.
Know and apply service-, model-, and aspect-oriented development approaches.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to course. Fundamental concepts
Hafta 2: Rationale for software architecture design
resources: Bass, L., Clements, P. and Kazman, R., "Software Architecture in Practice", ISBN: 321154959, Addison-Wesley, (2003).
Larman, C., "Applying UML and Patterns", ISBN: 0131489062, Prentice-Hall, (2004).

courses.name: COMPUTER VISION
courses.purpose: Bu ders, öğrencilerin bilgisayarla görmenin temel kavramlarını ve bu alandaki temel yöntemleri anlamalarını sağlamak için tasarlanmıştır. Görüntü oluşturma, temsil, analiz ve tanıma süreçlerine ilişkin kuramsal altyapı kazandırmayı amaçlar.
courses.description: Fundamental concepts of computer vision. Visual perception of human being. Mathematical foundations. Image formation and representation. Segmentation, feature extraction, contour and region analysis, camera geometry and calibration. Motion capture. 3-D reconstruction. Object and scene recognition. Object and people tracking. Human activity recognition and inference.
learning_outcomes: Know the fundamental concepts of computer vision.
Know the image formation and representation.
Learn the segmentation, feature extraction, contour and region analysis along with the camera geometry and calibration.
Comprehend the object and scene recognition.
Have an opinion about the human activity recognition and inference.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Fundamental concepts of computer vision.
Hafta 2: Visual perception of human being.
Hafta 5: Segmentation, feature extraction, contour and region analysis, camera geometry and calibration.
Hafta 11: Human activity recognition and inference.
Hafta 12: Human activity recognition and inference.
Hafta 13: Human activity recognition and inference.
Hafta 14: Human activity recognition and inference.

Parker J.R., Algorithms for Image Processing and Computer Vision, 2/E, ISBN: 0470643854, Wiley, 2010.

courses.name: MEDICAL INFORMATICS
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to the flow of medical information, medical business processes, and the standards used in medical information systems. It also aims to raise awareness of ethics, privacy, and security issues in medical informatics.
courses.description: Patient data. Electronic medical records. Coding standards. Clinical information systems. Hospital management informations systems. Medical decision support systems. Medical imaging technologies. Telemedicine. Ethics, privacy and security issues in medical informatics. Introduction to data mining for medical informatics. Machine learning for medical informatics.
learning_outcomes: Learn medical information flow
Learn medical business processes
Learn standards associated with medical information systems
Learn ethics, privacy and security issues in medical informatics

Hafta 4: Clinical information systems, Hospital management informations systems.
Hafta 7: Ethics, privacy and security issues in medical informatics
Hafta 9: Introduction to data mining for medical informatics
Hafta 10: Machine learning for medical informatics
Hafta 11: Machine learning for medical informatics
Hafta 12: Machine learning for medical informatics
Hafta 13: Machine learning for medical informatics

courses.name: GRAPH THEORY
courses.description: Graphs and graph models, connected graphs. Multigraphs and digraphs. Isomorphic graphs. Trees, the minimum spanning tree problem. Connectivity. Eulerian graphs. Hamiltonian graphs. Matchings and factorization. Planarity and planar graphs. Coloring graphs, the four color problem. Vertex coloring, edge coloring. Matrices and graph algorithms. Flows and cuts, constructing maximal flows.
learning_outcomes: Learn the basic concepts and techniques of graph theory.
Select appropriate models and algorithms of graph theory to solve the problems arising in computer science, engineering and operations research.
Learn Euler circuits and related application problems, solve them using algorithms.
Have theoretical knowledge on Hamilton circuits. Gain an understanding of the challenges arising in solving the traveling salesman problem.
Learn algorithms used for solution of the shortest path problem and compare their computational complexity.
Solve the problem of maximum flow. Implement the algorithms for matching and assignment problems.
Solve problems by applying the tree algorithms.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Graphs and graph models, connected graphs.
Hafta 4: Trees, the minimum spanning tree problem.
Hafta 10: Coloring graphs, the four color problem.
Hafta 13: Flows and cuts, constructing maximal flows.
Hafta 14: Flows and cuts, constructing maximal flows.
resources: Buckley F., Lewinter M., Friendly introduction to Graph Theory, Prentice Hall, 2003
Grimaldi R.P., Discrete and Combinatorial Mathematics, 5/E, Addison Wesley, 2003
Rosen K.H., Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications, 6/E, McGraw-Hill, 2007

courses.name: INTRODUCTION TO DATA MINING
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce students to data mining concepts and methods used in business settings, and to help them understand how data mining supports decision-making.
courses.description: Data mining in business; data mining processes and knowledge discovery; database support for data mining; overview of data mining techniques; cluster analysis; regression algorithms in data mining; neural networks in data mining; decision tree algorithms; linear programming-based methods; business data mining applications; market-basket analysis; text and web mining.
learning_outcomes: Learn Data Mining in Business.
Learn Data Mining Processes and Knowledge Discovery.
Understand Database Support to Data Mining.
Apply several algorithms of data mining techniques.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Data Mining Processes and Knowledge Discovery: Database Support to Data Mining;
Hafta 3: Overview of Data Mining Techniques;
Hafta 5: Regression Algorithms in Data Mining;
Hafta 6: Neural Networks in Data Mining;
resources: Turban E., Sharda R., Delen D., Decision Support and Business Intellegence Systems, 9/E, ISBN:9780132453233, Pearson, 2011
Olson D., Shi Y., Introduction to Business Data Mining, ISBN: 0072959711, McGraw Hill, 2007

courses.name: BIOINFORMATICS
courses.description: Global and Local Seuence Alignment. Statistical Significance. Suffix Tree and Suffix Array. Position Specific Scoring Matrix, Hidden Markov Model and Multiple Sequence Alignment. PhyloGenetic Trees. Protein Structures. Microarrays. Biological Networks.

Informed of Statistical Significance of Sequnce.
Learn Suffix Tree and Suffix Array.
Apply Position Specific Scoring Matrix, Hidden Markov Model and Multiple Sequence Alignment.
Informed of PhyloGenetic Trees.
Learn Protein Structures.
Informed of Microarrays.
Know Biological Networks.

courses.name: PARALEL VERİ İŞLEME
courses.purpose: Paralel programlama ve paralel işleme yaklaşımlarının temel kavramlarını, prensiplerini ve yapılarını öğretmek; öğrencilerin paralel algoritma tasarımı, analizi ve uygulaması konusunda yetkinlik kazanmasını sağlamak.
courses.description: Paralel programlama teknikleri, paralel işleme sistemlerinin sınıflandırılması ve paralel bilgisayar mimarileri ele alınır. Paralel bilgisayar modelleri, ileti aktarımı ile işleme, ardışık düzenim ile işleme, bellek paylaşımlı işleme gibi temel teknikler ile paralel işleme için algoritmalar ve uygulamalar incelenir.

Paralelleştirmeyi, prensiplerini ve yapılarını öğrenir.
Temel paralel makine yapılarını anlar.
Paralel algoritma tasarımı, analizi ve uygulamasını öğrenir.
Paralel işlemenin olası kısıtlarını anlar.

courses.name: PATTERN RECOGNITION
courses.purpose: An introduction to the machine recognition of one, two or higher dimensional patterns. It aims to provide students with the fundamental concepts and methods used in pattern recognition and to prepare them for applying these techniques in problem solving.
courses.description: Statistical and linguistic approaches to pattern recognition. Bayes Decision Theory, decision boundaries, classifiers and discriminant functions, estimation of parameters, clustering, feature selection, structural approaches to pattern recognition, neural network recognizers, and applications are covered.
learning_outcomes: Learn basic principles of pattern recognition
Learn machine learning methods
Get practice on developing and using PR programs
Get ability to PR techniques in probelm solving
weekly_topics: Hafta 11: Trend topics in pattern recognition - I
Hafta 12: Trend topics in pattern recognition - II
resources: Pattern Classification 2nd. Edition., R.O. Duda, P.E. Hart & D.G. Stork, J. Wiley Inc., 2001

courses.name: ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE
courses.description: Knowledge representation and reasoning. Syntax, semantics, and proof theory (deductive inference) of propositional logic. First-order predicate logic. Uncertainty. Probabilistic reasoning. Expert systems.
learning_outcomes: Learn the concept of Artificial Intelligence, its subjects and field of interests.
Get informed about problem solving techniques, knowledge representation and reasoning.
Learn propositional logic and how to program using first order logic.
Get informed about how to develop intelligent and self learning systems by understanding machine learning concept.
Have background infromation about supervised and unsupervised classification techniques
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence Concept.
Hafta 6: Logic Concept, Proposition and inference.
Hafta 10: Machine Learning and General Concepts.
Hafta 14: Introduction to Artificial Neural Networks.

Vasif V. Nabiyev, 2010, Yapay Zeka, Seçkin Yayıncılık, 3. Basım, Ankara.

courses.name: MULTIMEDIA SYSTEMS
courses.description: Introduction to multimedia and digital technology. Fundamental concepts of multimedia. History of multimedia. Hypermedia and multimedia. Multimedia authoring and tools. Multimedia data types and representations. Color science, color in image and video. Fundamental concepts of digital audio and video. Lossy and lossless data compression. JPEG image compression. MPEG-1 and MPEG-2 coding. MPEG-7 multimedia content description standard. Traditional vs content-based retrieval systems and sample applications.

Know digital image formats and properties.
Know digital audio and video formats and properties.
Describe lossy compression methods.
Describe lossless compression methods.
Apply JPEG coding method.
Know MPEG coding standards.
Know storage techniques of multimedia data in databases.

courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the fundamental concepts and methods used in constrained and unconstrained optimization, as well as numerical techniques commonly applied in engineering and computing problems.

Learn linear, non-linear and square programming.
Learn various algorithms and applications.

courses.name: UNIX SYSTEMS PROGRAMMING
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a solid understanding of UNIX operating system fundamentals and the principles behind system-level programming. It also develops practical skills for writing shell and kernel-level programs and working with concurrent processes.
courses.description: UNIX fundamentals. Shell programming. Processes and threads. Concurrency and synchronization. Inter-process communication.
learning_outcomes: Learn fundamental concepts of UNIX operating systems
Understand and explain the mechanisms of system processes and threads
Understand and implement concurrency
Get practice on developing shell and kernel-level programs
resources: UNIX Systems Programming, Robbins and Robbins, 2003, First Ed.

courses.name: REAL-TIME SYSTEMS
courses.description: Introduction to real-time systems. Reliability and fault tolerance. Exceptions and exception handling. Concurrent programming. Shared variable-based synchronization and communication. Message-based synchronization and communication. Atomic actions, concurrent processes and reliability. Resource control. Programming real-time abstractions. Scheduling real-time systems. Programming schedulable systems. Tolerating timing faults. Low-level programming.
learning_outcomes: Learn real time systems.
Understand real time operating systems concepts.
Learn task switching, time attunement and data communication concepts.
Understand real time software development methods.
Learn real time software development tools.
weekly_topics: Hafta 5: Shared variable-based synchronization and communication.
Hafta 7: Atomic actions, concurrent processes and reliability.
resources: Burns A., Wellings A., Real-Time Systems and Programming Languages, 3/E, Addison Wesley, 2001
Jane W., Liu S., Real-Time Systems, Prentice Hall, 2000.
Cottet F., Scheduling in real-time systems, John Wiley and Sons, 2002

courses.purpose: This course introduces specific research topics selected from new or emerging areas of Computer Science.

Gain experience in applying their theoretical knowledge to a new research area
Understand the importance of lifelong learning process in Engineering
